title: New measurement of the neutron-induced total cross- sections of natCr in a wide energy range on the Back-n at CSNS

x4freetext: New measurement of the neutron-induced total cross-
sections of natCr in a wide energy range on the
Back-n at CSNS

x4codes.hlp: Institute of High Energy Physics, CAS, Beijing, China, People`s Republic
Southwest Inst.Nucl.Phys.and Chem.,Mianyang,Sichuan, China, People`s Republic

x4codes.hlp: Institute of High Energy Physics, CAS, Beijing, China, People`s Republic

x4freetext: The back-streaming white neutron
beamline (Back-n) at the China Spallation Neutron
Source (CSNS)
The proton beam current was in double-bunch
mode, the average proton hitting power was 125 kW.
In each pulse, there were two proton bunches
approximately 30 ns wide (rms) with a 410 ns interval.
A 15 mm thick natural chromium sample, with purity of
99.99%, diameter of 70 mm, mass of 416.5 g and
density of 7.26 g/cm3.
A multi-layer fast fission chamber consisted
of high purity fission cells, front and rear neutron
windows, and a drum cavity. Four cells of 235U and
four cells of 238U were arranged in the multi-layer
fast fission ionization chamber for low-energy and
fast-energy neutron detection, respectively. The time
resolution was better than 6 ns.
Neutron flight path of four different 235U
fission cells were 77.7380 m, 77.7566 m, 77.7768 m
and 77.7966 m. Because 238U fission cells are not
sensitive to low-energy neutrons, the flight
distances of four 238U fission cells were obtained by
increasing the 20 mm distance sequentially.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainty primarily arises from the
atomic density of the sample, the statistical
uncertainty in neutron counting and the fission
events.
